speaker: Unknown
en: So come up with your values when you're getting a little bit of traction.
zh: 所以当你获得一点牵引力时，想出你的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason I say that is early enough where you... And the way you come up with the values is not like, what values should we have as philosophers?
zh: 我之所以这么说，是因为要足够早，以至于你……而你提出价值观的方式不是像，作为哲学家我们应该有什么价值观？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, no.
zh: 不，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should figure out why are we being successful?
zh: 你应该弄清楚我们为什么成功？

speaker: Unknown
en: Literally write down the 5 to 10 things that are the reasons you are being successful and those become your values and you kind of read.
zh: 字面上写下 5 到 10 件你们正在成功的原因的事情，这些就成了你的价值观，你有点在读。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we did that.
zh: 所以我们就这样做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so our first one was going to speed above everything.
zh: 所以我们的第一点是速度高于一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was us being fast.
zh: 那是我们变得很快。

speaker: Unknown
en: The second one is never disappoint the customer.
zh: 第二点是永远不要让客户失望。

speaker: Unknown
en: Technical mastery, high output matters.
zh: 技术精通，高产出很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the way down to ones that are not obvious, like laugh a lot.
zh: 一直延伸到那些不明显的，比如多笑一笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's been our core value from at the beginning of the company's history.
zh: 从公司历史开始以来，这一直是我们的核心价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like when you're working on intense things, if you don't have the ability to keep grounded, have perspective, laughter and humor also is a way to get subtle feedback and a slightly different taste than this sucks.
zh: 就像是当你处理激烈的事情时，如果你没有保持脚踏实地、拥有视角的能力，笑声和幽默也是一种获得微妙反馈的方式，以及一种与这很糟糕略有不同的品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can say, "It's not the best."
zh: 你可以说，「这不是最好的。」

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is slightly... And so you're really creating the framework in which people are learning how to behave with each other within the company.
zh: 那是稍微……所以你真的在创建一个框架，人们在其中学习如何与公司内部的人相处。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so today the values really serve us as almost like a... They're like guiding principles.
zh: 所以今天价值观真的作为……就像指导原则为我们服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we do new team meetings that every week, Peter and I, where we meet all the new team members, and we're almost always just talking about the values and some level of detail and depth.
zh: 所以我们要和 Peter 和我一起举办新团队会议，每周，我们会见所有的新团队成员，我们几乎总是只谈论价值观以及某种程度的细节和深度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Another value, half the work is follow-up.
zh: 另一个价值观，一半的工作是跟进。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just taking notes and following up.
zh: 只是记笔记和跟进。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the business.
zh: 这就是生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not more complex than that.
zh: 没有比这更复杂的了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Laugh a lot is my new favorite company value.
zh: 多笑一笑是我最喜欢的新公司价值观。

speaker: Qasar Younis
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sounds like a wonderful place to work.
zh: 听起来这是一个工作的好地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then on the last piece there, there's this book that just came out by Stripe Press about maintenance and how valuable and underappreciated the maintenance part of work is.
zh: 然后在最后一部分那里，有一本 Stripe Press 刚出版的关于维护的书，以及工作中维护部分是多么有价值却被低估。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely.
zh: 绝对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Absolutely.
zh: 绝对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if there's a takeaway that you get from, let's say, a bit of my philosophy on where we started the conversation around why... Being promotional has all these negative connotations in it, but so I'm careful using that word, but why not be promotional?
zh: 我认为如果要总结一个收获，比如说，从我的哲学中得出一点，也就是我们要开始对话时围绕为什么……推广这个词有所有这些负面含义，所以我很谨慎地使用这个词，但为什么不进行推广呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's because there's costs to everything.
zh: 这是因为每件事都有成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if you can focus on the craft and making the product really, really good and really listening to your customers, you have a much higher likelihood of success.
zh: 所以如果你能专注于技艺，把产品做得真的非常非常好，并且真正倾听你的客户，你有高得多的成功可能性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you can always then go and scale there.
zh: 然后你总是可以去那里进行扩张。

speaker: Unknown
en: A part of that is the thing that you're talking about.
zh: 那其中一部分就是你所谈论的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maintenance or another version of... My roots are in automotive engineering, and automotive engineering is actually exercising quality.
zh: 维护或者是另一个版本……我的根基在于汽车工程，而汽车工程实际上是在实践质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're building these very complex machines at scale.
zh: 你正在大规模地建造这些非常复杂的机器。

speaker: Unknown
en: People talk about rockets being really, really complex.
zh: 人们谈论火箭真的非常复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: You only got to set up a rocket even at the highest, once every couple of days.
zh: 你即使以最高频率，也只能每几天设置一次火箭。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're making a car every 30 seconds and you have to make it extremely cheap and it's globally competitive.
zh: 你每 30 秒制造一辆车，而且你必须让它非常便宜，并且它是全球竞争的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you really get into the nuance and minutiae of how a factory runs.
zh: 所以你真的深入到工厂如何运行的细微细节和琐事中。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a factory is about safety and maintenance.
zh: 工厂是关于安全和维护的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not a lot of complex things.
zh: 没有很多复杂的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like when you break down what is being operationally strong, operationally strong is keeping an eye on a handful of things and make sure you're doing them really, really good.
zh: 这就像当你拆解什么是在运营上强大时，运营上强大就是关注少数几件事，并确保你把它们做得真的非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm one of those believers that there's this adage, "A man who cannot command himself is not fit to command others."
zh: 我是这句话的信徒，“不能指挥自己的人不适合指挥他人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that maintenance aspect is a part of that.
zh: 而维护方面是其中的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, well, if you maintain yourself and your own work, you maintain your team, you maintain the company, the products are almost, they come out of all of that system.
zh: 这就像，好吧，如果你维护你自己和你自己的工作，你维护你的团队，你维护公司，产品几乎是，它们是从那个系统中产生出来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think a lot of founders don't think about their company as a system or almost as a machine.
zh: 我认为很多创始人不把他们的公司看作一个系统或者几乎是一台机器。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I would implore you to do that because then you really focus on the craft of the machine and building the machine and making it more hygienic and making it more well-tuned.
zh: 但我要恳求你这样做，因为那时你真正专注于机器的工艺，构建机器，让它更卫生，让它更调试良好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like you'll meet people who really love cars and they really obsess about the maintenance of cars.
zh: 就像你会遇到真正爱车的人，他们真的痴迷于汽车的维护。

speaker: Unknown
en: They will detail underneath the driver's seat as somebody who details my own cars.
zh: 他们会清理驾驶座下面，作为一个我自己清理自己车的人。

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's going to create gravity within Silicon Valley to influence founders to then make certain types of companies.
zh: 这将在硅谷产生引力，影响创始人去创建特定类型的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then of course they invest in those.
zh: 然后当然他们投资于这些公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: On this thread about reading to find almost areas you disagree and haven't thought about, I know one of your approaches to management and one that maybe of value is to encourage your leaders to listen to naysayers to not create this positive reinforcement cycle.
zh: 关于这个通过阅读来寻找你不同意且未曾思考过的领域的线索，我知道你的一种管理方法，可能很有价值的方法是鼓励你的领导者倾听反对者，不要创造这种正向强化循环。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk about why that's so important, how you operationalize that.
zh: 谈谈为什么这如此重要，你是如何将其落地的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So imagine I'm not the founder of the company and Peter's not the co-founder.
zh: 所以想象一下我不是公司的创始人，Peter 也不是联合创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just a generic company.
zh: 只是一家普通公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: The ideal situation with a generic company is one that you can put in lots of different competing ideas.
zh: 普通公司的理想情况是你可以把许多不同的竞争想法放进去。

speaker: Unknown
en: The culture is one where you'll shake those ideas out.
zh: 这种文化是你能把这些想法筛选出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not an emotion in it.
zh: 里面没有情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whoever brings the idea, the best idea wins.
zh: 无论谁提出想法，最好的想法获胜。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why can't companies do that?
zh: 那为什么公司不能那样做呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Frankly speaking, a lot of times it's the founders.
zh: 坦白说，很多时候是创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the founders are told by popular media and the way that human beings experience life and our tribal kind of outlook, that you have to have this hard view.
zh: 而且创始人受大众媒体以及人类体验生活和部落式世界观的方式所影响，认为你必须有这种强硬的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everyone has, if they're not following you, then maybe you're a weak leader or something like that.
zh: 而且每个人都有这种想法，如果他们不跟随你，那么你可能是一个软弱的领导者或者类似的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we just don't believe that.
zh: 我认为我们只是不相信那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't just believe in that philosophy.
zh: 我们不只是相信那种哲学。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think we believe that what maybe a more tactical way of saying that is we take inputs of the environment, our customers, specifically, our employees, our competitors, of our investors, and of what's happening in society write large, and that impacts our strategy.
zh: 我认为我们相信的是，也许用更战术性的方式来说，我们要接收来自环境的输入，具体来说是我们的客户、我们的员工、我们的竞争对手、我们的投资者，以及整个社会正在发生的事情的输入，这影响了我们的战略。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's one of the reasons we've been very, very successful.
zh: 我认为这是我们非常非常成功的原因之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not so arrogant to think that we just have the answers because we had the ambition to start a company.
zh: 我们不会傲慢地认为仅仅因为我们有创办公司的野心就有了答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that permeates into a very specific culture.
zh: 我认为这渗透成了一种非常特定的文化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the culture that we've built is one, it's also not being contrarian for contrarian's sake.
zh: 我认为我们建立的文化是，也不是为了逆向而逆向。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, one view I do have is emotions are generally not helpful in making rational decisions.
zh: 嗯，我的一个观点是，情绪通常对做出理性决策没有帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're almost like the opposite of it.
zh: 它们几乎是理性的对立面。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes passion and leadership are supposed to be about... They're supposed to be, again, magnanimous or emotional.
zh: 有时激情和领导力应该是关于……它们应该再次表现为宽宏大量或情绪化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we just don't believe that.
zh: 我们只是不相信那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's, I think, a bit more of our kind of Midwest roots showing for Peter and I. And so, I think it's not that we disagree with everything in the room.
zh: 我认为这更多是展现了 Peter 和我的中西部根源。所以，我认为不是我们不同意房间里的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: What we specifically say is speak up, speak up.
zh: 我们具体说的是畅所欲言，畅所欲言。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone has to speak up because that one person with their one experience, because they worked at Zoox, or Waymo, or wherever, Tesla, or they worked at a Chinese company, whatever it is.
zh: 每个人都必须发言，因为那一个人带着他那一个经验，因为他们在 Zoox 或 Waymo 或 Tesla，或者一家中国公司工作过，无论是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: That one idea they have in their head when the debate is happening about what we should do in space, literally the space of space, and something maybe we don't know much about.
zh: 当我们正在讨论我们在空间领域应该做什么，字面意义上的太空空间，以及可能我们知之甚少的事情时，他们脑海中的那一个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: That one person's one idea, they have to feel comfortable sharing it.
zh: 那一个人的那一个想法，他们必须感到舒适地去分享它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if they're the most junior person or they feel that they didn't get their way in the last debate or they feel whatever anxiety that they might have, they have to share that opinion that, guys, this is actually the right idea or this is the wrong idea.
zh: 即使他们是资历最浅的人，或者他们觉得在上一场辩论中没有如愿，或者他们感到任何焦虑，他们必须分享那个意见，伙计们，这实际上是正确的想法或者是错误的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you can create that environment where the best idea wins.
zh: 如果你能创造那个最好的想法获胜的环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: Gandhi has this line, truth is what stands as the test of time.
zh: 甘地有一句话，真理是经得起时间考验的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think there's become a little bit of a meme in the Bay Area like truth seeking as a culture, but it kind of is like that.
zh: 我认为湾区有点把求真作为一种文化变成了一个梗，但确实有点像那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're trying to find the best idea.
zh: 我们试图寻找最好的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe truth is the wrong word, maybe it's the best idea.
zh: 也许真理是错误的词，也许是最好的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Find the best idea and then let's go full bore against the best idea.
zh: 找到最好的想法，然后让我们全力以赴去实现最好的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's maybe use a counterfactual.
zh: 也许我们用一个反事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why do companies fail when they have great talent and they have seemingly all the same components that an Applied Intuition have?
zh: 为什么当公司拥有优秀的人才并且拥有看起来和 Applied Intuition 相同的所有组件时却失败了？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's because maybe the best ideas are not being surfaced and certainly maybe they're not actually being adopted.
zh: 这是因为也许最好的想法没有被浮出水面，当然也许它们实际上没有被采纳。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or more often than not, when I think about companies that have been very successful is they have momentum going in a specific direction and that wall of sound overwhelms any new sound that's emerging which is, "Hey, the market's changing, the market's changing."
zh: 或者更常见的是，当我想到那些非常成功的公司时，它们有一个向特定方向发展的势头，那堵声音之墙淹没了任何正在出现的新声音，也就是，「嘿，市场正在变化，市场正在变化。」

speaker: Unknown
en: You just can't even hear that because there's all this momentum going in a particular direction.
zh: 你甚至听不到那个，因为有所有这些势头朝着特定方向发展。

speaker: Unknown
en: A good example, I had front row seats for this when I worked at Google, it was the era where Facebook was emerging.
zh: 一个很好的例子，我在 Google 工作时对此有前排座位，那是 Facebook 正在崛起的时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: And here's Google, and people don't remember Google in the late 00s and early teens.
zh: Google 在这里，人们不记得 2000 年代末和 2010 年代初的 Google。

speaker: Unknown
en: Google wasn't just a company.
zh: Google 不只是一家公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was the apex predator of Silicon Valley.
zh: 它是硅谷的顶级掠食者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Apple was just the MacBook Air had just come out.
zh: Apple 只是一家 MacBook Air 刚刚出来的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Steve Jobs was getting in the right direction, but nothing like Apple is today.
zh: Steve Jobs 当时正在朝着正确的方向前进,但完全不像今天的 Apple。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazon, AWS was still a young thing.
zh: Amazon,AWS 还是个年轻的事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nvidia was teetering off of bankruptcy.
zh: Nvidia 正在破产的边缘摇摇欲坠。

speaker: Unknown
en: All these giant companies that you think of, Microsoft was run by Balmer, Twitter was a small thing.
zh: 所有这些你想到的巨头公司,Microsoft 是由 Balmer 运营的,Twitter 是个小东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: But Google was this already larger than life, number one company.
zh: 但 Google 已经是个比生活更巨大的、排名第一的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody wanted to work at Google, and there were not many companies with that stature.
zh: 每个人都想在 Google 工作,没有多少公司有那样的声望。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then in the periphery, this little company Facebook starts emerging.
zh: 然后在边缘地带,这家小公司 Facebook 开始崭露头角。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Google, who has the best engineers on the planet, is making a billion in cash flow a month, tries to fight this little company.
zh: Google,拥有地球上最好的工程师,每月产生 10 亿美元的现金流,试图对抗这家小公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I remember Facebook at that time maybe had 1,000 people, and Google was like 15X, 20X the size with a lot of cashflow.
zh: 我记得那时的 Facebook 大约只有 1000 人,而 Google 的规模是其 15 倍、20 倍,拥有大量的现金流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And why couldn't Google fight Facebook?
zh: 为什么 Google 打不赢 Facebook?

speaker: Unknown
en: It's because Google is not Facebook.
zh: 因为 Google 不是 Facebook。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like the Confucian saying, how does a gorilla learn how to fly?
zh: 这就像那句儒家格言,大猩猩是如何学会飞行的?

speaker: Unknown
en: By not being a gorilla.
zh: 通过不再做大猩猩。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way that Google would've won the social media wars by being a social media company, it's just fundamentally not.
zh: Google 想要赢得社交媒体战争的唯一途径是成为一家社交媒体公司,但它根本上就不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so this happens in companies all the time, which is you're just going in one direction with momentum consciously or unconsciously because that's where all the employees are, that's what the culture is, that's what they work on.
zh: 这种情况在公司中一直发生,你只是沿着一个方向凭借惯性行进,无论是有意还是无意,因为所有的员工都在那里,那就是文化所在,那就是他们正在做的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then something changes in the market and you just can't even move there.
zh: 然后市场发生了某些变化,而你根本无法转向那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that also can happen or surprisingly at really small companies.
zh: 我认为这种情况也可能,甚至令人惊讶地,发生在非常小的公司里。

speaker: Unknown
en: So where founders have a view and it's like that view is the view it's going to be.
zh: 在那里创始人们有一个观点,而那个观点就是既定的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And actually that can be just 10 degrees off from what was the correct path.
zh: 实际上,那个观点可能只偏离了正确路径 10 度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the whole company's kind of led astray.
zh: 整个公司都被带偏了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were in the right market.
zh: 他们处在了正确的市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: They might've even been solving the right problem, but they were just a little off.
zh: 他们甚至可能正在解决正确的问题,但他们只是稍微有点偏离。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're so scared of failing and so scared of losing that I will humble myself and listen to other people and they say, "Hey, we're five degrees off course here."
zh: 因为我们太害怕失败,也太害怕失去,所以我会让自己谦卑下来,倾听其他人的意见,他们会说,“嘿,我们偏离航向 5 度了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, okay, let's maybe fix the course.
zh: 然后就像,好吧,让我们修正一下航向。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then once that becomes your culture, then it's really hard to lose because everybody's not about fulfilling a preset path.
zh: 一旦这成为了你的文化,那就真的很难输掉,因为大家不是在 fulfilling 一条预设的路径。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just about finding how to win.
zh: 他们只是在寻找如何获胜。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is exactly what I wanted to ask about.
zh: 这正是我想问的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone listening to this either is like, "Oh yeah, we're very open-minded.
zh: 每一个听到这个的人都会说,“噢是的,我们非常思想开明。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're absolutely going to listen to everyone's opinions and decide rationally the right path in practice."
zh: 我们绝对会听取每个人的意见,并在实践中理性地决定正确的路径。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Almost never happens.
zh: 但这几乎从未发生过。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: Right。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or they're just like, "We know we're not good at this.
zh: 或者他们就像,“我们知道我们不擅长这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just too nice to each other."
zh: 我们对彼此太好了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you do this at a company that isn't good at this?
zh: 你如何在一个不擅长这个的公司里做到这一点?

speaker: Unknown
en: Does it have to be the CEO top down in your experience?
zh: 根据你的经验,这必须由 CEO 自上而下地进行吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: Does it have to be part of the culture?
zh: 这必须成为文化的一部分吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you operationalize at a company that's not like you?
zh: 你如何在一个不像你的公司里将其落地?

speaker: Unknown
en: The middle way is typically the right way.
zh: 中道通常是正确的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's hard to find the middle way because these are conflicting ideas.
zh: 很难找到中道,因为这些是相互冲突的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: The guardrails or the posts you just set.
zh: 你刚才设置的护栏或标杆。

speaker: Unknown
en: One side is like, we're just going to go on the other side as we're too maybe almost unsure.
zh: 一边就像,我们就这样继续吧,另一边是我们可能太……几乎不确定了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you have to somehow, once you do have that debate, you have to then confidently walk down that path.
zh: 你必须以某种方式,一旦你完成了辩论,你就必须自信地沿着那条路径走下去。

speaker: Unknown
en: And again, this is conflicting.
zh: 同样,这是冲突的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just said, be humble enough to listen to what's going on.
zh: 我刚才还说,要足够谦卑去倾听正在发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then once that decision is made, the decisive... In our values, that first value, the speed value, which is specifically the wording is move fast, move safe.
zh: 但一旦那个决定做出后,就是那种果断……在我们的价值观中,第一个价值观,速度价值观,具体的措辞是 move fast,move safe。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's specifically the wording.
zh: 具体的措辞就是那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: We assess our managers on adherence to those values.
zh: 我们根据对这些价值观的遵守情况来评估我们的管理者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Literally, we compensate and promote against those values.
zh: 字面意义上,我们的薪酬和晋升是对照这些价值观来决定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're not just abstract values.
zh: 它们不仅仅是抽象的价值观。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the behavior we're actually looking at under speed is decisiveness.
zh: 所以我们在速度这一项下实际上关注的行为是果断性。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're setting up a system that is looking at these conflicting kind of things.
zh: 所以我们正在建立一个着眼于这些冲突事物的系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is be open and the other one's make decisions quickly and you have to hold those intention.
zh: 一个是要开放，另一个是要快速做出决策，你必须兼顾这两种意图。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is why you as the co-founder or founder of the company get paid the big bucks.
zh: 这就是为什么作为公司的联合创始人或创始人，你能拿高薪。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to do that.
zh: 你必须做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to know when to bluff and when to hold them and when to fold them, as I say.
zh: 你必须知道什么时候虚张声势，什么时候保留筹码，什么时候弃牌，正如我所说。

speaker: Unknown
en: So at some point, and that point comes sometimes faster than you think, you will not get any more information and you have to make a decision.
zh: 所以在某个时刻，这个时刻的到来有时比你想象的要快，你将无法获得更多信息，你必须做出决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're walking this very, very thin line.
zh: 所以你在走一条非常非常细的界线。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your point about emotions was extremely interesting, and I want to make sure people don't take away the wrong takeaway here.
zh: 你关于情绪的观点非常有趣，我想确保人们不要从这里得出错误的结论。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I actually found really helpful, which I think is aligned with what you were suggesting is taking emotions out of it.
zh: 所以我发现真正有用的，我认为这与你的建议一致，就是将情绪从中剥离。

speaker: Unknown
en: The way I've used this in my work is when you have to make a hard decision, pretend nobody's feelings would be hurt and emotions are not involved.
zh: 我在工作中使用这种方法的方式是，当你必须做出艰难决定时，假装没有人的感情会受到伤害，也不涉及情绪。

speaker: Unknown
en: What would you do if nobody cared?
zh: 如果没人在乎你会怎么做？

speaker: Unknown
en: If they're like, "Totally great."
zh: 如果他们说，“完全没问题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So what would you do in that world?
zh: 那么在那个世界里你会怎么做？

speaker: Unknown
en: And then that tells you, okay, that's actually the right thing to do.
zh: 然后那会告诉你，好吧，这实际上是正确的做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it's, okay, how do I help people feel okay about this?
zh: 然后就是，好吧，我如何帮助人们对此感觉良好？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do I deal with the downsides of this path?
zh: 我如何处理这条路径的负面影响？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think that's the obvious version of it.
zh: 是的，我认为这是显而易见的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think maybe another version of thinking is it's an emotion is, let's take another route.
zh: 我认为也许另一种思维版本是，它是一种情绪，让我们换条路走。

speaker: Unknown
en: You, as the leader or as the engineer who is getting a direction, you already have some preset view that this is my idea.
zh: 你，作为领导者或作为接受指令的工程师，已经预设了这是我的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's an emotional construct and it's around ownership and feeling of ownership.
zh: 那是一种情感构念，它围绕着所有权和所有权的感觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, I really fall into that category of the more... So even maybe most fundamentally, what is an emotion?
zh: 所以是的，我真的属于那一类，即更……所以也许最根本的是，什么是情绪？

speaker: Unknown
en: An emotion is these set of reactions that a framework that's been imparted in your brain through life experiences.
zh: 情绪是一组反应，是大脑通过生活经验输入的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those life experiences might not actually may have not been optimized for you to make a decision in a product review.
zh: 而那些生活经验可能实际上并没有为了你在产品评审中做决策而进行优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the more you can pull that away, a good heuristic would be the same decision being made by multiple people in the company gets the same result.
zh: 所以你能更多地剥离它，一个好的启发式方法是公司里的多个人做出同样的决定会得到同样的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you're removing a little bit of that almost like filter.
zh: 所以你正在移除一点那种几乎像滤镜的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can almost think of that emotion as a filter.
zh: 你几乎可以把那种情绪想象成一个滤镜。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I like to have the raw image come through, the raw decision come through so we can consistently classify it again and again, not to get too abstract, but I don't know if that makes sense.
zh: 所以我喜欢让原始图像通过，让原始决定通过，这样我们可以一次又一次地一致地对其进行分类，不要太抽象，但我不知道这是否有道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it makes sense.
zh: 是的，有道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I have one last question, but there's an interesting trend I've noticed with people talking about AGI.
zh: 我有最后一个问题，但是我注意到人们谈论 AGI 时有一个有趣的趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: The missing piece I've been hearing more and more is just emotions are what creates consciousness potentially.
zh: 我越来越多地听到缺失的一环仅仅是情绪，它可能是创造意识的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Michael Pollan has a new book out about consciousness and his take is it's not just more intelligence, it's actually emotions that led to the consciousness.
zh: Michael Pollan 有一本关于意识的新书，他的观点是不仅仅是更多的智力，实际上是情绪导致了意识。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's, let's say, undermining how complex human thought is to think that it's just, let's say, the inputs and outputs of, or let's say association, for the lack of better word, of ideas, facts, words, letters.
zh: 我认为这可以说是低估了人类思维的复杂性，如果认为它只是想法、事实、单词、字母的输入和输出，或者为了找不到更好的词，联想。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not just as associations, and creativity is kind of a little bit of that as well.
zh: 不仅仅是联想，创造力也有点像那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: The old saying of technical mastery is mastering the complex, and I think computers do that really well, and creativity is mastering the simple.
zh: 俗话说，技术精通是掌握复杂，我认为计算机做得很好，而创造力是掌握简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure I'm going to eat my words on this as the best artist in three years.
zh: 我敢肯定三年后作为最好的艺术家我会收回我说的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's beautiful.
zh: 这很美。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's beautiful.
zh: 这很美。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, yeah.
zh: 是的，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think, and this again goes back to my philosophy of consume broad inputs, but then try to remove that filter, see things as honestly as they possibly can, create a culture in the company that is also similar and doesn't put any weight on who the idea came from or where it came from.
zh: 所以我认为，这又回到了我的哲学，即消费广泛的输入，然后尝试移除那个滤镜，尽可能诚实地看待事物，在公司里创造一种类似的文化，不重视想法来自谁或它来自哪里。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then, ultimately, as a leader, you decide, and by the way, you got to be right.
zh: 但最终，作为领导者，你做决定，顺便说一句，你必须是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the other thing I think a lot of founders just, we don't emphasize enough.
zh: 这是另一件事，我认为很多创始人只是，我们没有足够强调。

speaker: Unknown
en: Founders love to take credit for things.
zh: 创始人喜欢为事情邀功。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just human nature.
zh: 这只是人性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody does.
zh: 每个人都这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the reality is you have to be right.
zh: 但现实是你必须是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not enough to just start a company.
zh: 仅仅创办一家公司是不够的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not enough to have this vision of the world.
zh: 拥有这种世界观是不够的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to be right.
zh: 你必须是对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the evidence is, is the company is a sustainable standalone business, because we're talking specifically about venture-backed AI companies in Silicon Valley.
zh: 证据是，公司是一家可持续的独立业务，因为我们专门谈论硅谷的风险投资支持的 AI 公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of my, but I should have said this at the beginning, all of my advice is specifically for that narrow group, which is founders of AI venture-backed companies in the Bay Area.
zh: 我所有的，但我应该在开始时就说这个，我所有的建议专门针对那个狭窄的群体，即湾区 AI 风投支持公司的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of that, last question.
zh: 说到这个，最后一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been wanting to get to this because it's an interesting spicy take that you have as the last question.
zh: 我一直想问这个问题，因为作为最后一个问题，你有一个有趣的、带有辛辣意味的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know you have to run after this.
zh: 我知道这之后你就得跑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have this view that a lot of CEOs in Silicon Valley don't actually have great taste.
zh: 你有这样的观点，即硅谷的许多 CEO 实际上并没有很好的品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm excited to hear what your experience there and just what you think.
zh: 我很兴奋听到你在那方面的经验，以及你的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I also want to be careful to imply that I do.
zh: 我也想小心不要暗示我有。

speaker: Unknown
en: I fall into that group.
zh: 我也属于那一类人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it is true because a lot of the... Couple of reasons, both taste in the most, let's say, artistic sense and in the most running a company and what should be the HR policy for point X. A lot of that is I think they're just not exposed to a lot of interesting good things.
zh: 我认为这是真的，因为很多……有几个原因，既有在最艺术意义上的品味，也有在经营公司以及在 X 点应该实行什么样的人力资源政策方面的品味。我认为其中很多只是因为他们没有接触过很多有趣的、好的事物。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's been a theme in this whole conversation is like, just get more and more exposure.
zh: 而这一直是整个对话的主题，就像，只是获得越来越多的接触。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very unfortunate when I meet somebody who, and I'm not particularly thinking of anyone in particular.
zh: 当我遇到某人时，这很不幸，我没有特别想到任何特定的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if this is you and you're one of my friends, I apologize.
zh: 所以如果这是你，而且你是我的朋友之一，我道歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not talking about you, but it's like you grow up in Cupertino, you go to Berkeley, and the first thing you do when you come out of school is you start a company and then that's all you do for 20... You've never even been an employee.
zh: 我不是在说你，但就像你在 Cupertino 长大，你去 Berkeley，你离开学校后做的第一件事就是创办一家公司，然后你就做这件事做了 20 年……你甚至从来没有当过员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: And why I think that's so important.
zh: 以及为什么我认为那如此重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I spent over a decade working in large organizations and truly large, more than 100,000 employees, like a General Motors or like a Bosch.
zh: 我在大型组织中工作了十多年，真正的大型组织，超过 10 万名员工，比如 General Motors 或者 Bosch。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you're in the back alley of that organization, the bowels of those organizations, you learn how bad it is to be an employee.
zh: 当你在这种组织的后巷，这些组织的内部深处时，你会了解到作为一名员工是多么糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: The bureaucracy above you, leadership doesn't know what's going on, the industry, your antiquated tools, all this stuff.
zh: 你头顶的官僚主义，领导层不知道发生了什么，行业，你过时的工具，所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why that's so important to experience as an individual is then when you become a leader, you're making policies and you're creating culture and you have to keep that in mind.
zh: 为什么作为个人体验这一点如此重要，是因为当你成为领导者时，你在制定政策，你在创造文化，你必须把这一点记在心里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a bunch of founders, we just never had, frankly, the fortune of being at the bottom of the totem pole.
zh: 许多创始人，坦率地说，我们只是从未有过处在图腾柱底层的运气。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's just one version of how to... That doesn't obviously seem like consume the photos of Bresson or Picasso or whoever it might be, but it's something similar.
zh: 这只是关于如何……的一种版本……这显然不像是去欣赏 Bresson 或 Picasso 或任何人的照片，但它是类似的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's something similar about, you can sometimes meet founders, and maybe a good heuristic here is there's some founders that would be good at lots and lots of things, actually not just being a founder of an AI company in the Bay Area.
zh: 这其中有某种相似之处，你可以有时遇到一些创始人，也许这里有一个好的启发法，有一些创始人在很多事情上都会很擅长，实际上不仅仅是成为湾区的 AI 公司的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's something about taste there, because really what you're talking about is understanding humans and understanding life, and then being able to discern with some judgment what is good and what's not good, because that's really what we're talking about.
zh: 这其中有关于品味的东西，因为你真正谈论的是理解人类和理解生活，然后能够用某种判断力去辨别什么是好的，什么是不好的，因为这就是我们真正在谈论的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if your life experience is very narrow, you could still be good or have the ability to discern what's good and what's not good.
zh: 所以如果你的生活经验非常狭窄，你仍然可以很好，或者有能力辨别什么是好的，什么是不好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think there's something about if you've backpacked for a few years around the world, I somehow believe that's going to be a better founder.
zh: 但我认为如果你背着背包在世界各地旅行几年，这会有某种作用，我不知何故相信那会成为一个更好的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, I don't know how I can... There's no peer-reviewed research that I can point to that says that.
zh: 就像是，我不知道我怎么……没有我可以指向的同行评审研究说那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that's what I'm getting at.
zh: 所以我想这就是我要表达的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is some developing of taste.
zh: 这里有某种品味的培养。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I feel like we have helped people build their taste, feed their model with more insights and different perspectives in this conversation.
zh: 好吧，我觉得我们帮助人们建立了他们的品味，用更多的洞察力和不同的视角在这个对话中喂养他们的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like we could chat for hours, but I know you got to run.
zh: 我觉得我们可以聊几个小时，但我知道你得跑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I'm not sure if there are any real takeaways other than that.
zh: 是的，我不确定除了那个之外是否有任何真正的要点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, zero.
zh: 好的，零。

speaker: Unknown
en: We really went everywhere.
zh: 我们真的到处都聊到了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sorry if you had a particular line of questions you wanted to go down.
zh: 如果你有一条特定的想要深入的问题线，我很抱歉。

speaker: Unknown
en: We went in all the perfect directions.
zh: 我们去了所有完美的方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, good.
zh: 好的，很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Good, good.
zh: 很好，很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Qasar, thank you so much for doing this.
zh: Qasar，非常感谢你做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for being here.
zh: 非常感谢你的到来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Final question, just where can folks find you online.
zh: 最后一个问题，大家在网上哪里可以找到你。

speaker: Unknown
en: How can listeners be useful to you?
zh: 听众怎么能帮到你？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a great question.
zh: 这是一个很好的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love to hear what are books that I don't know.
zh: 我喜欢听到我不知道的书。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's always good.
zh: 所以这总是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of my favorite books have been just randomly recommended to me, so I'll take that.
zh: 我最喜欢的一些书只是随机推荐给我的，所以我会接受那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, I consume research as well.
zh: 当然，我也消费研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if there's something particularly novel that's going on, obviously all the mainstream stuff we as a company and me as an individual are going to consume, but things that are a bit off the beaten path, we're always looking for that.
zh: 所以如果有特别新颖的事情发生，显然所有主流的东西，我们作为一家公司和我作为一个个人都会消费，但是那些有点偏离正轨的事情，我们总是在寻找那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, and then if you have a particular opinion about specifically our domain, physical AI and how AI is going to impact mines, farms, construction sites, robotax, all of that stuff, I'm always interested to hear new opinions on that, or even old opinions maybe with a different viewpoint.
zh: 但是是的，如果你对特别是我们的领域有特定的看法，物理 AI 以及 AI 将如何影响矿山、农场、建筑工地、robotax，所有这些东西，我总是有兴趣听到关于那个的新观点，或者是旧观点也许带有不同的视角。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yeah, if you see me, you can see me online.
zh: 所以是的，如果你看到我，你可以在网上看到我。

speaker: Unknown
en: Of course, I'm always around as well.
zh: 当然，我也总是在周围。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm always open to that feedback.
zh: 我总是对那种反馈持开放态度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're on Twitter now.
zh: 而且你现在在 Twitter 上了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There you go.
zh: 就是这样。

speaker: Qasar Younis
en: Yeah, exactly. Yeah. Follow me there. Yeah, exactly. That's the answer, follow me there.
zh: 是的，没错。是的。在那儿关注我。是的，没错。这就是答案，在那儿关注我。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the call to action.
zh: 这就是行动号召。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm channeling my inner Marc.
zh: 我正在唤起我内心的 Marc。

speaker: Unknown
en: There you go.
zh: 就是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Qasar, thank you so much for doing this and for being here.
zh: Qasar，非常感谢你做这些并来到这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, thanks for having me.
zh: 是的，谢谢你的邀请。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was a lot of fun.
zh: 这非常有意思。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bye, everyone.
zh: 再见，大家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you so much for listening.
zh: 非常感谢大家的收听。

zh: 如果你觉得这很有价值，你可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或你最喜欢的播客应用上订阅本节目。

en: Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review as that really helps other listeners find the podcast.
zh: 另外，请考虑给我们评分或留下评论，因为这真的能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。
